##NAMES=Postite
##RRUFFID=R250094
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Mg(H_2_O)_6_Al_2_(OH)_2_(H_2_O)_8_(V_10_O_28_)·13H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Blue Cap mine, Lyon Canyon Creek, San Juan County, Utah, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Divergent and “jackstraw” masses of parallel bundles of very thin, needle-like golden-yellow prisms with pyramidal terminations. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2012)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250094
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Mg_0.97_Na_0.06_Ca_0.04_Sr_0.01_K_0.01_)Al_1.94_[(OH)_2_(H_2_O)_0.08_](V_10_O_28_)·27H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 16.3357 b: 24.2434 c: 11.7343 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 4647.2 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
